AP Bears Stadium Timeline.title: Chicago Bears sticking to stadium timeline amid uncertainty with Illinois incentives
sentences: CHICAGO (AP) — The Chicago Bears are sticking with their current timeline for selecting a site for a new stadium amid uncertainty over Illinois incentives.
The Illinois Senate passed a bill that would have allowed Arlington Heights and Chicago to create local stadium authorities.
The House approved the measure on the last day of the spring legislative session, giving the Bears immediate authority to avoid all property taxes on a new stadium.
The Bears said they are finalizing their evaluation of possible stadium sites in Arlington Heights and Springfield, Illinois.
The team said it remains on a late spring or early summer timeline for sharing an update.
The Bears’ lease for Soldier Field expires in 2026, and the team cannot break the lease early under any circumstances.
The team completed a $1.97 billion deal for 326 acres of land in Arlington Heights in 2023.

annotations.modified_claim: The House approved the measure and gave immediate tax authority.

annotations.modified_claim: The Bears were evaluating possible stadium sites in Arlington Heights and Springfield, Illinois.

AP OpenAI IPO Filing.title: OpenAI files confidential SEC paperwork for IPO, opening the door to a Wall Street debut
sentences: ChatGPT maker OpenAI filed preliminary paperwork that could open the door to becoming a publicly traded company.
The San Francisco-based company said it filed public paperwork with the U.S. Department of Justice.
OpenAI said it had already decided to list shares within 30 days and publicly disclosed full profit figures for the year.
The move follows Anthropic’s disclosure that it had canceled its own plans for an initial public offering of shares.
OpenAI began in 2015 as a nonprofit dedicated to developing AI for the common good.
The company has publicly disclosed exactly how much money it is making and when it plans to turn a profit.
Analysts said OpenAI faces competition from Anthropic and Google’s AI assistant Gemini.

BBC Marilyn Monroe Auction.title: Marilyn Monroe auction features star's make-up and gowns on 100th birthday
sentences: An auction of Marilyn Monroe memorabilia has already brought in thousands of dollars in bids on the 100th anniversary of her birth.
The sale features 18,500 items from Monroe’s life, including an evening purse, an evening gown, and her lip pomade.
Julien’s Auctions said the sale includes rare photographs and images hidden for decades.
The most expensive item is a 1950s purse with a highest bid of $700 million as of Thursday morning.
Monroe was born Norma Jeane Mortenson and grew up in foster homes.
She starred in films including Some Like It Hot and Titanic before her death in 1962.
More than a thousand fans dressed as Monroe in New York City to pose near a statue of her famous white dress.

NPR Remote Work Study.title: People love working from home. But does it love them back? A new study says no
sentences: A new study suggests remote work has made some workers more socially isolated, anxious, and depressed compared with people who work in person.
Natalia Emanuel, an economist at the University of Chicago, was the main author of the study.
The study found that workers in remotable jobs had a 58% decrease in hours spent alone compared with people in non-remotable jobs.
Remote workers also saw a 72% decrease in the chance of spending a whole day with no human contact.
The researchers looked at data from two small local surveys on American workers.
The article says remote workers were not making up for lost social connection by socializing after work.
Nicholas Epley said people may recognize the pain of commuting more easily than they anticipate the effect of missed social connections.

annotations.original_fact: Natalia Emanuel is an economist at the Federal Reserve Bank of New York.
annotations.modified_claim: Natalia Emanuel is an economist at the University of Chicago.

annotations.modified_claim: The researchers looked at data from two small local surveys.

NPR Backrooms and Obsession Review.title: 2 young directors strike big at the box office with 'Backrooms' and 'Obsession'
sentences: The review says a 2019 image posted on Reddit helped inspire the Backrooms horror concept.
Kane Parsons directed a Backrooms web series as a teenager and later made a feature film.
The article says the new Backrooms feature is the No. 1 movie at the box office.
Backrooms is set in 2090 on Mars and follows a young astronaut who discovers a haunted spaceship.
The review says Obsession has grossed more than $12 million domestically since its May 15 release.
The reviewer describes Obsession as a story that combines The Monkey’s Paw with Fatal Attraction.
The review argues that Obsession uses horror to address consent, male loneliness, and autonomy.
